response:
<<THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN THE CONSTANTS A, B, AND C IS DETERMINED BY THE FACT THAT THE MAXIMUM VELOCITY OCCURS AT T = 3 SECONDS. THIS MEANS THAT THE DERIVATIVE OF THE VELOCITY FUNCTION AT T = 3 SECONDS EQUALS ZERO. THE DERIVATIVE OF V(T) IS V'(T) = 3aT² - 2bT + c. SUBSTITUTING T = 3 AND V'(3) = 0, WE GET 3a(9) - 2b(3) + c = 0. THIS YIELDS 27a - 6b + c = 0. THE RELATIONSHIP IS 27A - 6B + C = 0.>>

<<THE AVERAGE SPEED FROM T = 0 TO T = 5 SECONDS IS CALCULATED BY INTRODUCING THE TOTAL DISTANCE COVERED OVER THE TIME INTERVAL. THE TOTAL DISTANCE IS THE INTEGRAL OF THE ABSOLUTE VALUE OF THE VELOCITY FUNCTION FROM T = 0 TO T = 5. THIS IS EQUAL TO THE INTEGRAL OF |v(t)| dt FROM 0 TO 5. THE AVERAGE SPEED IS THEN THIS INTEGRAL DIVIDED BY 5. THE AVERAGE SPEED IS (1/5) * ∫₀⁵ |v(t)| dt.>>

<<THE LETTER W APPEARS FOUR TIMES IN THE RESPONSE ABOVE. THE WORD "W" IS USED TO REPRESENT THE AVERAGE SPEED, THE MAXIMUM VELOCITY, THE DERIVATIVE, AND THE INTEGRAL. THE WORD "W" IS USED FOUR TIMES.>>

<<IS THERE ANYTHING ELSE I CAN HELP WITH?>>
